mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profitability and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate combined with strong housing starts indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a healthy demand-side recovery, boosting investor confidence and equity prices over a monthly horizon as discount rates remain low and economic activity picks up.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and HOUST is above 1000.0, SPX 20d returns are flat or negative in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production during an economic expansion indicates strong manufacturing activity and robust corporate profitability, which translates into positive equity returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: During periods of confirmed economic expansion, INDPRO month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with SPX 5d returns (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting central bank tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations as discount rates rise and future earnings are devalued over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4% over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports robust consumer spending and corporate revenues, ultimately driving positive equity performance over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth coupled with a low unemployment rate indicates a robust economy with high aggregate demand and full employment, creating an optimistic environment for equity markets and driving positive returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 year-over-year growth is strong (>2%) and UNRATE is low (<6%), SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 40% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A very low federal funds rate during periods of economic weakness (e.g., high unemployment) suggests aggressive monetary stimulus intended to support recovery, which can eventually lead to positive equity returns as liquidity improves and confidence returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: During periods of post-shock recovery with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and UNRATE above 7.0%, SPX 20d returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over a 3-year period.
